mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is particularly useful for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep technical knowledge. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and potential resource demands depending on the complexity of the AI agents being built.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This sets up your kubeconfig for the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys Flowise on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/Flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml --namespace flowise

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Flowise deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the Flowise application.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Flowise to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp flowise-backup:/data /local/backup --namespace flowise

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl get pods --namespace flowise

```

> Backup your Flowise data.
> Upgrade Flowise to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "flowise:3.0.13": rpc error: code = NotFound'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified image version does not exist in the container registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image version and ensure it is correctly specified in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on Flowise pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace flowise' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Flowise pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models, improved UI for agent creation, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your existing agents to the new model format.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all Flowise pods in 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
